channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ayushman Bharat Sanctions ₹1.73 Lakh Crore for Hospital Treatments Benefiting Millions
item.description: As of 28th February 2026, a total of 11.69 crore hospital admissions across India, amounting to ₹1.73 lakh crore, have been sanctioned under the Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-...

item.title: Funding constraints leave many Tanzanian secondary schools without science laboratories
item.description: The article explains how limited government funding is slowing down the construction and completion of science laboratories in Tanzania’s government secondary schools, especially in rural areas such a...

item.title: Iran seeks to play World Cup 2026 matches in Mexico due to safety concerns
item.description: Iran football federation is discussing with FIFA to move its World Cup 2026 matches from the United States to Mexico because of worries about the safety of its players. Mehdi Taj, president of Iranian...

item.title: PNP-ACG advises public to watch out for online scams during Lenten season and rising costs
item.description: The Philippine National Police - Anti-Cybercrime Group (PNP-ACG) has issued a warning to the public to stay vigilant against online scams, especially as fuel, travel, and product prices continue to ri...

item.title: Pakistan clarifies airstrikes targeted Taliban military sites, not Kabul hospital
item.description: The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting has rejected claims by the Afghan Taliban that Pakistan targeted a hospital in Kabul. The ministry clarified that the strikes were aimed at 'Camp Phoenix',...

item.title: Bank of Africa Niger Reports Sharp Profit Drop Amid Rising Loan Risks
item.description: Bank of Africa Niger don report say their net profit for 2025 don fall sharply by 92 percent, according to their board statement from 20 February 2026. The bank talk say dis drop na because the whole ...

item.title: Heathrow Terminal 4 experiences significant drop in passenger numbers amid Middle East flight cancellations
item.description: Heathrow Airport's Terminal 4 has seen a dramatic reduction in passenger numbers, losing approximately one-third of travellers since the US-Israeli attack on Iran on 28 February. The terminal, primari...

item.title: Live Updates on the U.S.-Israeli Military Campaign in Iran and Regional Fallout
item.description: This live news page provides continuous updates on the fast‑moving war involving Iran, the United States, and Israel, focusing on military operations, regional reactions, and global economic consequen...

item.title: Body of Health Officer Found After Lake Tanganyika Boat Accident
item.description: The body of Amos Sika, aged 33, a former health department officer, was recovered on March 15, 2026, in Kalalangabo village, Kigoma District, after going missing in Lake Tanganyika. He had been on a b...

item.title: Indonesian activist suffers acid attack amid concerns over military influence in civilian affairs
item.description: Indonesian authorities are investigating a brutal acid attack on Andrie Yunus, a prominent activist known for his criticism of the military’s growing involvement in civilian affairs. The assault occur...

item.title: NBA Rejects FCT Judge’s Order For Lawyer To Kneel, Calls It Unlawful

item.title: Wickes announces expansion to 300 UK stores, creating over 2,000 jobs
item.description: Wickes, the UK-based DIY retailer, has revealed plans to expand its network to 300 stores nationwide, generating more than 2,000 new jobs. Currently operating 230 sites, the company aims to open four ...

item.title: Tanzania Cricket Association announces squad for ICC Men’s T20 World Cup Africa Qualifier
item.description: The Tanzania Cricket Association (TCA) has officially named its squad for the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup Sub-Regional Africa Qualifier B, set for March 22-30 in Accra, Ghana. This tournament is part of t...

item.title: Pakistani tanker navigates Hormuz Strait on route cleared by Iran amid regional conflict
item.description: A Pakistani-flagged tanker, named Karachi, has successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz following a route approved by Iran, signalling Tehran’s control over safe passage amid ongoing regional confl...

item.title: Community Pantry Supports Nova Scotia Veterans Coping with Rising Living Expenses
item.description: In Nova Scotia, a local community pantry has been established to support veterans who are struggling with the rising cost of living. Many former service members face financial challenges due to inflat...

item.title: Nkosi Champions Early Childhood Development and Inclusive Fatherhood in South Africa
item.description: Siphesihle Perfect Nkosi is making significant strides in South Africa’s Early Childhood Development (ECD) sector through his initiative, Isiqalo. Focused on under-resourced communities, Isiqalo equip...

item.title: Daikin Launches Air Creator Lineup for Personalized Home Air Comfort
item.description: Daikin Philippines has introduced its new residential Air Creator lineup, aimed at giving users full control over their indoor air environment. The lineup includes three main products: the Air Creator...

item.title: President Tinubu Departs Abuja for State Visit to United Kingdom Hosted by King Charles III
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, together with im wife di First Lady Senator Oluremi Tinubu, don comot Abuja on Tuesday, March 17, 2026, to begin one historic two-day state visit to di United Kingdom. Dis...

item.title: Nvidia Forecasts $1 Trillion in Orders for Its Blackwell and Vera Rubin AI Chips
